Infrastructure Overview

Son of Anton

A distributed local LLM mesh network that researches, learns, and reports autonomously — so you don't have to be at the keyboard for the work to continue.

3

Active Nodes

6

LLM Models Loaded

7

Research Streams

24/7

Autonomous Operation

Network Topology

via Tailscale mesh

Current Fleet

Three machines on a Tailscale mesh, synced via Syncthing, coordinated by cron.

Travel Yes (mobile)

The human interface. Runs Claude Code and Software of You directly. Syncs data bidirectionally with all nodes.

The next machine entering the mesh — a significant GPU upgrade.

Legion

Lenovo Legion 16" OLED

GPU RTX 5080 16GB GDDR7
CPU Ultra 9 275HX
RAM 32GB DDR5-6400
Storage 1TB Gen5 NVMe
Display OLED 2560x1600 240Hz
WiFi Wi-Fi 7 BE 320MHz

Impact on the Mesh

VRAM Capacity Comparison
Razer — RTX 3060 6 GB
Lucy — RTX 3080 Ti 12 GB
Legion — RTX 5080 16 GB GDDR7
Combined mesh total 34 GB
Larger Models

16GB GDDR7 can run 30B+ parameter models fully in VRAM. Qwen 2.5 32B, Mixtral, or fine-tuned specializations.

Gen5 Architecture

GDDR7 memory bandwidth and PCIe Gen5 storage mean dramatically faster model loading and inference throughput.

Tier Flexibility

Powerful enough to handle any tier. Could replace Razer as Tier 1, promote Lucy to Tier 1, or run a dedicated synthesis tier.

Dual-Use

OLED 240Hz display + full GPU makes this a portable workstation and gaming rig when not running inference.

How It Works

Three tiers of intelligence, running on schedules, producing knowledge autonomously.

T1

Web Research Sweeps

Mistral 7B on the Razer scans the web for new developments across all research streams.

Every 6 hours
Mistral 7B @ ~57 tok/s
Filters signal from noise
T2

Summarization & Synthesis

Qwen 2.5 14B on Lucy processes Tier 1 findings into living wiki documents.

Every 12 hours
Qwen 2.5 14B @ ~72 tok/s
Updates wiki documents
T3

Cross-Stream Intelligence

Claude CLI runs overnight, synthesizing across all streams into weekly intelligence digests.

Overnight at 3am ET
Claude via CLI subscription
Weekly "coffee shop" digest
Web Sweep Filter & Score Summarize Wiki Update Cross-Synthesize Weekly Digest

Active Research Streams

Seven concurrent research streams, prioritized by project activity. The system watches what you work on and shifts focus accordingly.

Hephaestus / Spec-Site P8

Automated spec-site generation, design tokens, Cloudflare ecosystem, output homogeneity problem

Entropy / Game Dev P7

UE5, PaperZD, HD-2D rendering, indie game development patterns

Voice Acting Craft P6

Indie VO trends, voice direction, casting landscape, ACTRA developments

UE5 Technical Skills P6

Blueprints, PaperZD tutorials, animation state machines, level design

GrimSparkle / Design P5

Soulslike design philosophy, gothic aesthetic, dark fantasy art direction

FF Lore & Narrative P4

Final Fantasy lore systems, JRPG narrative design, Ultimania reference

Horror Memorabilia P2

Market trends, collectibles, slower cadence background stream

Capabilities

What Son of Anton does while you're away from the keyboard.

Telegram AFK Interface

Talk to your data from anywhere

A Telegram bot runs on the Razer 24/7, backed by Claude CLI. Query your contacts, check project status, log interactions, or ask questions about your data — all from your phone. The system processes your message through the full SoY database and responds conversationally.

Continuous Learning

Research that evolves with your work

The ambient research pipeline observes your active projects and adjusts research priorities automatically. Working on Spec-Site? Hephaestus stream gets bumped. Starting a new UE5 project? Game dev research intensifies. The system learns what matters to you and shifts focus — no manual configuration needed.

Living Wiki Documents

Knowledge that grows autonomously

Each research stream maintains a wiki document that's continuously updated by Tier 2 synthesis. These aren't static notes — they evolve as new findings come in, old information gets refined, and cross-stream connections are discovered. Currently maintaining 7 wikis with 3,800+ words of synthesized intelligence.

Self-Healing Infrastructure

Detects problems, fixes what it can, alerts you for the rest

The health monitor runs every 30 minutes, checking SSH connectivity, Ollama status, model availability, and task completion rates. If Ollama goes down, it auto-restarts it. If a machine goes unreachable, it alerts you via Telegram with diagnostics. Failed research tasks get flagged before they pile up.

Bidirectional Sync

Every machine sees the same data

Syncthing keeps the codebase and SQLite database synchronized across all nodes in real-time. Work on the Mac, and the Razer sees it instantly. Research findings from Lucy propagate to every machine. The system is designed so writes are temporally separated — Tier 1 and Tier 2 never write simultaneously — avoiding SQLite conflicts.

Weekly Intelligence Digest

Your personal research briefing

Every week, Claude synthesizes all research streams into a single intelligence digest — key developments, cross-stream insights, and a hands-on "This Week's Workshop" learning module calibrated to your skill levels. Think of it as a personal research assistant that reads everything so you don't have to.

Data Architecture

Everything is local. Nothing leaves your network unless you tell it to.

Storage

Single SQLite database, synced to all machines. Research findings, wiki documents, task history, and activity logs all live in one file. No cloud database, no external services.

Privacy

All LLM inference happens on your hardware. Research queries go through local models. Only Tier 3 synthesis uses Claude via subscription — and that's optional. Your data never trains anyone else's model.

Networking

Tailscale mesh VPN connects all machines with encrypted WireGuard tunnels. No port forwarding, no public IPs. Machines find each other automatically, even across networks.

Operating Cost

$0

Tier 1 & 2 inference

Local models, your hardware

$0

Tier 3 synthesis

Claude CLI on existing subscription

$0

Networking & sync

Tailscale free tier + Syncthing OSS

The entire system runs on hardware you already own, software that's free, and a Claude subscription you're already paying for.

Son of Anton · Generated
